elements.title: Grimskär
elements.contents: ... that the island Grimskär (pictured) was probably used for executions of criminals, whose bodies were left there on display as a warning to passing ships?

elements.title: Jan Opaliński
elements.contents: ... that the wedding of Jan Opaliński became infamous due to several accidents and quarrels?

elements.title: Bull Believer
elements.contents: ... that the lyrics of "Bull Believer" were inspired by Cocaine & Rhinestones and contain references to Mortal Kombat and Augustine of Hippo?

elements.title: Muriel Wilson
elements.contents: ... that soprano Muriel Wilson stated that her ability to sing was impaired after her skull was fractured in a car accident?

elements.title: MacBook Neo
elements.contents: ... that the MacBook Neo uses a processor found in iPhones?

elements.title: 2026 Lake Tahoe avalanche
elements.contents: ... that the 2026 Lake Tahoe avalanche was the deadliest in the United States since 1981?

elements.title: Vinson Cunningham
elements.contents: ... that Vinson Cunningham based his novel Great Expectations on his work for Barack Obama's presidential campaign?

elements.title: Romanian lăutari violin
elements.contents: ... that some forms of the Romanian lăutari violin have extra strings that are added only for resonance and never played directly?

elements.title: a convicted bank robber argued
elements.contents: ... that a convicted bank robber argued that a restitution law could not apply to him because it did not exist when he committed the crime?
